name: United States

visas.name: H-1B Specialty Occupation
visas.description: For workers in specialty occupations requiring at least a bachelor's degree. Subject to annual cap (65,000 + 20,000 for US master's holders). Selected by lottery.
requirements: Job offer from a US employer
Bachelor's degree or equivalent in a specialty field
Labour Condition Application (LCA) approved by DOL
visas.leadsTo: EB green card (employer must sponsor separately)

visas.name: EB-1 Priority Workers Green Card
visas.description: Green card for people with extraordinary ability (EB-1A), outstanding professors/researchers (EB-1B), or multinational managers (EB-1C). No employer sponsor needed for EB-1A.
requirements: EB-1A: Evidence of extraordinary ability (awards, publications, high salary, critical role)
EB-1B: Employer sponsor + 3 years experience + international recognition
EB-1C: Employer sponsor + 1 year as manager/executive abroad

visas.name: EB-5 Investor Visa
visas.description: Green card through investment. Minimum $800,000 in a Targeted Employment Area (TEA) or $1,050,000 elsewhere. Must create 10 full-time jobs for US workers.
requirements: Investment in a new commercial enterprise
Create or preserve 10 full-time jobs for US workers

visas.name: Family-Based Green Card
visas.description: Green card sponsored by a US citizen or permanent resident family member. Immediate relatives of US citizens (spouse, parents, unmarried children under 21) have no annual cap.
requirements: Qualifying family relationship to US citizen or LPR
Sponsor must meet income requirements (125% of poverty level)
visas.processingTime: 12 months–20+ years (depends on category and country of birth)

visas.name: Asylum
visas.description: Protection for people who have suffered persecution or have a well-founded fear of persecution based on race, religion, nationality, political opinion, or membership in a particular social group.
requirements: Must apply within 1 year of arriving in the US
Must demonstrate persecution or credible fear of persecution
Cannot be barred (e.g. serious crime, security threat)
visas.maxDuration: Indefinite (leads to green card after 1 year)
visas.leadsTo: Green card after 1 year, citizenship after 5 years

citizenship.name: Naturalization
citizenship.requiresResidency: 5 years as LPR (3 years if married to US citizen)

benefits.childBenefit: Child Tax Credit — up to $2,000 per child under 17

taxNotes: US taxes worldwide income for all residents and citizens. Must file FBAR if foreign accounts exceed $10,000. FATCA applies to foreign financial assets over $50,000.
